title: Designing a Full-Stack System
concept: A worked walkthrough designing a multi-agent system with memory, RAG, and MCP for a product requirement.

exercises.prompt: What is an AI agent designed to do?
exercises.explain: An AI agent is designed to perform tasks autonomously, making decisions based on data and programming.

cards.title: What is a Full-Stack System?
cards.body: A Full-Stack System includes both front-end (user interface) and back-end (data management) components, which work together seamlessly.

cards.title: Multi-Agent System
cards.body: A Multi-Agent System is a system where multiple AI agents interact to solve problems, each contributing unique abilities or information.

cards.title: Team Sports Analog
cards.body: Think of a Multi-Agent System like a soccer team. Each player has different skills (like offense and defense) that help the team win.

cards.title: Real-World Example
cards.body: Consider a delivery service where different agents track packages, update customers, and route drivers. Each agent performs its part to ensure the service runs smoothly.

cards.title: Myth: AI Can Work Alone
cards.body: Many people think AI can solve problems alone, but most successful AI systems work via collaboration among multiple agents.

cards.title: Components of a Full-Stack System
cards.body: A Full-Stack System integrates memory, retrieval, and management for efficient operation.

exercises.prompt: What is the role of memory in a Full-Stack System?

options.text: To help agents learn and adapt

exercises.explain: Memory helps agents learn from past experiences, making them smarter and more effective in future interactions.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A Multi-Agent System can operate independently without any data sharing.
exercises.explain: False. A Multi-Agent System relies on data sharing and collaboration among agents to function effectively.

exercises.prompt: In a Full-Stack System, agents rely on ____ to adjust their actions based on past experiences.
exercises.sentence: In a Full-Stack System, agents rely on memory to adjust their actions based on past experiences.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'memory' because it allows agents to learn and adapt over time.

exercises.prompt: Which component allows agents to find relevant information quickly?
exercises.explain: Retrieval and Generation (RAG) is the component that allows agents to find the relevant information quickly from memory.

exercises.prompt: What is the importance of integrating memory, RAG, and management in designing a Full-Stack System?

options.text: To create a system that operates independently
To enable agents to learn and collaborate effectively
To minimize the number of agents required
To avoid data retrieval at all costs

exercises.explain: The correct answer is that integrating these components enables agents to learn and collaborate effectively, leading to improved decision-making and problem-solving.
